seeneyu_relevance: seeneyu's core loop (Watch → Record → AI Feedback → Repeat) is a direct implementation of deliberate practice: each session has a well-defined goal (mimic a specific skill), immediate feedback (AI scoring), and structured repetition. This thread establishes that expert performance is built through this exact mechanism.
hypothesis: Structured, feedback-driven repetition — not raw talent — produces measurable skill improvement. seeneyu users who practice regularly will demonstrate quantifiable improvements in body language metrics.
key_finding: Deliberate practice with immediate feedback explains 23-48% of performance variance across domains. The absence of feedback is the single biggest predictor of practice ineffectiveness.
evidence_grade_summary: B/D — foundational theoretical framework (Grade D) supported by empirical replication studies (Grade B/C). Contested on effect size but not on core mechanism.

sources.title: The role of deliberate practice in the acquisition of expert performance
sources.key_finding: Expert performance is the product of structured practice with immediate feedback, not innate talent. Practice alone (self-regulated, goal-directed, with feedback) explains up to 48% of performance variance.
sources.seeneyu_application: The Watch → Record → AI Feedback → Repeat cycle is a direct operationalization of deliberate practice. Each clip session has a defined skill goal, behavioral execution, and immediate scored feedback.
sources.quote: Deliberate practice involves the provision of immediate feedback, time for problem-solving and evaluation, and opportunities for repeated performance to refine behavior.
sources.limitation: Original effect size (48%) was not replicated in double-blind study; revised estimate ~23-26%.

sources.title: The role of deliberate practice in expert performance: revisiting Ericsson, Krampe & Tesch-Römer (1993)
sources.key_finding: Pre-registered replication with double-blind procedures found deliberate practice explains 26% of performance variance (vs. 48% in original). The core mechanism is real, but effect size is more modest than originally claimed.
sources.seeneyu_application: 26% is still a strong, clinically meaningful effect size for a behavioral intervention. Practice remains the strongest modifiable predictor of skill improvement.
sources.quote: We did not replicate Ericsson et al.'s major result of complete correspondence between the skill level of the groups and their average accumulation of practice time alone with the violin.

sources.title: Peak: Secrets from the New Science of Expertise
sources.key_finding: Refined deliberate practice model: mental representations are built through practice-feedback-correction cycles. Without feedback, 'naive practice' produces plateaus, not improvement.
sources.seeneyu_application: AI feedback prevents users from plateauing. Users without feedback (e.g., free-tier self-practice) will stagnate; users with AI scoring will keep improving — this differentiates paid tiers.
sources.quote: Without feedback — either from yourself or from outside observers — you cannot figure out what you need to improve on or how close you are to achieving your goal.

sources.title: Deliberate practice and performance in music, games, sports, education, and professions: A meta-analysis
sources.key_finding: Meta-analysis of 88 studies: deliberate practice explains 26% of performance variance in games, 21% in music, 18% in sports, 4% in education, 1% in professions. Domain matters — motor and performance skills respond most strongly to deliberate practice.
sources.seeneyu_application: Body language is a motor/performance skill (same category as music and sports) — the highest-responding category. This is the strongest evidence category for seeneyu's approach.
sources.quote: Deliberate practice explained 26% of the variance in performance for games, 21% for music, 18% for sports.

sources.title: Deliberate practice and acquisition of expert performance: A general overview
sources.key_finding: Deliberate practice requires: (1) a well-defined task with known correct technique, (2) immediate informative feedback, (3) repetition with correction opportunities. All three conditions must be present.
sources.seeneyu_application: seeneyu satisfies all three conditions: reference clip = correct technique, AI scoring = immediate feedback, repeat sessions = repetition with correction.
